varieties.name: Reilly 🌱
varieties.traits: Low chill, medium-large nut, light brown, free-falling
varieties.growing_notes: SPECIFICALLY DEVELOPED FOR SUBTROPICAL BRISBANE. Indigenous Australian selection from Alstonville near Brisbane. Low chill performs well in subtropical conditions. Requires well-drained soil to minimize Phytophthora.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (Kyogle, NSW), Subtropical Fruit Club of Qld

varieties.name: George Sands (Alstonville) 🌱
varieties.yield_notes: Sets well alone, benefits from cross-pollination
varieties.growing_notes: Local Alstonville selection performing well in subtropics. Indigenous to Brisbane's subtropical region. Adapted to warm, humid conditions with proper drainage.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Queensland specialty nurseries

varieties.name: April Gold
varieties.origin: Dandenong Ranges, Victoria (early selection, European origin)
varieties.traits: Extra large dark colored nuts, upright habit, free-falling

varieties.name: Japanese Chestnut (Seedling)
varieties.traits: Small to medium tree (10-15m), spreading, tolerates heat and humidity
varieties.growing_notes: BEST HEAT TOLERANCE of all chestnuts. Grown successfully in subtropical Brazil. Naturally resistant to blight and ink disease. MOST SUITABLE SPECIES for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Barely deciduous in warm climates. Tolerates summer heat and humidity better than European types.

varieties.name: Crane
varieties.growing_notes: Blight resistant. Heat and drought tolerant. SUITABLE for Brisbane with proper drainage.

varieties.name: Sleeping Giant
varieties.growing_notes: Highly blight resistant (Chinese heritage). MARGINAL for Brisbane - prefers cooler but worth trial in cooler microclimates.

varieties.name: Colossal
varieties.growing_notes: Blight resistant. Hybrid vigor provides some heat adaptation. POTENTIAL for Brisbane but untested in true subtropical conditions.

varieties.name: Moreton Bay Chestnut (Black Bean)
varieties.origin: Queensland coast (Moreton Bay), Northern NSW, Indigenous to Brisbane
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree (35-45 ft cultivated), beautiful pinnate leaves, red-yellow flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Ornamental tree, seeds traditionally used by Aboriginal peoples after processing
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS TO BRISBANE REGION. Species: Castanospermum australe (NOT true chestnut - Fabaceae family). Native to tropical and subtropical Queensland. Perfectly adapted to Brisbane climate. Aboriginal food source for thousands of years. Seeds contain toxic saponins requiring traditional processing. Valuable timber. Now susceptible to shot hole borer. Not for casual consumption - traditional knowledge required.
varieties.availability: Australian native plant nurseries, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Malabar Chestnut (Guiana Chestnut)
varieties.traits: Semi-deciduous tree, spectacular red-brown flowers, large woody pods
varieties.yield_notes: Precocious, heavy cropping in subtropical areas
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR BRISBANE SUBTROPICAL. Species: Pachira insignis/glabra (NOT true chestnut - Malvaceae family). Tolerates frost inland from Bundaberg. Grown successfully in Brisbane area. Frost-free location preferred. Full sun to partial shade. Well-drained soil. Requires frequent watering. Not a true chestnut but produces edible nuts after proper preparation.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, tropical plant nurseries

varieties.name: Singhara / Singara (Water Chestnut) 🏆
varieties.origin: Indigenous to India - traditional crop in Bihar, West Bengal, Jharkhand, Kerala backwaters
varieties.traits: Aquatic floating plant, grows in ponds/wetlands, black/green horned nuts with 2-4 spines
varieties.yield_notes: 4-12 tonnes/hectare. Bihar Large Red: 7 t/ha. Green Spineless: up to 12 t/ha
varieties.growing_notes: Species: Trapa natans (Lythraceae - aquatic annual, NOT a tree). EXCELLENT FOR KERALA WETLANDS/BACKWATERS. Indigenous traditional Indian crop. Grows in tropical/subtropical freshwater. Called 'cooling food' - beats summer heat. Used medicinally (anti-diabetic, anti-inflammatory). Thrives in monsoon. Farmers grow fish for half year, then water chestnuts. pH 6.7-8.2 ideal. Planted at monsoon beginning. Traditional Ayurvedic use. Made into flour, used fresh. Perfectly adapted to Kerala's wetland agriculture. Can be integrated with traditional paddy field/backwater farming systems.

varieties.name: Chinese Water Chestnut
varieties.origin: China, Southeast Asia, grown in parts of India
varieties.traits: Rush-like plant, produces underground tubers (corms), brown chestnut-colored skin
varieties.growing_notes: Species: Eleocharis dulcis (Cyperaceae - sedge/rush, grows tubers). SUITABLE for Kerala aquatic cultivation. Grows in freshwater ponds. Different from Singhara (Trapa) - this is tuber not nut. Used extensively in Asian cooking. Crisp texture retained when cooked. High in fiber. Good for tropical wetland farming.
varieties.availability: Asian vegetable suppliers, specialty aquatic plant nurseries

varieties.name: Indian Chestnut (Java Olive)
varieties.origin: Native to India, Southeast Asia. Indigenous to Kerala and Western Ghats
varieties.traits: Large tropical deciduous tree, big leathery leaves, unique star-shaped fruit pods
varieties.growing_notes: Species: Sterculia foetida (Malvaceae family). EXCELLENT FOR KERALA/THIRUVALLA. Indigenous to India including Kerala. Perfectly adapted to tropical monsoon climate. Fast-growing, drought-resistant after establishment. Pest-resistant. Valuable timber (durable wood). Traditional medicine use (bark, leaves, seeds - Ayurvedic). Seeds edible after roasting. Used in local cuisines. Thrives in Kerala's heat and humidity. Important reforestation tree. Soil conservation (extensive roots). Cultural significance - gathering spot, sometimes sacred. Well-adapted to monsoon rainfall patterns.
varieties.availability: Greenverz, Kerala Forest Department nurseries, Indian native plant suppliers

varieties.name: Malabar Chestnut (Pachira)
varieties.origin: Central/South America, widely grown in India including Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu (Western Ghats)
varieties.traits: Evergreen tropical tree (up to 50 ft), glossy dark green leaves, spectacular white flowers, semi-deciduous
varieties.growing_notes: Species: Pachira insignis/glabra (Malvaceae family). EXCELLENT FOR KERALA/THIRUVALLA. Grown extensively in Western Ghats of Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu. Perfectly adapted to warm tropical climate with plenty of rainfall - ideal for monsoon regions. Best harvested in dry season (ripe fruits). Well-suited to Thiruvalla's conditions. Requires warm climate, can tolerate some frost. Prefers full sun to partial shade. Well-drained soil essential. Used for desserts, snacks. Quality nuts have glossy bright-brown color. Can be stored 2 months cool/dry, or frozen 6 months. Ornamental value (beautiful flowers). Seeds raw not recommended (prepare first).
varieties.availability: Kerala nurseries, Western Ghats plant suppliers, tropical nurseries throughout India

varieties.name: 2. Indian Chestnut (Sterculia foetida) - Native Kerala species
